title: Register of Street-door Book

sections.body:
10:15 Decroix in / 11:40 out. 13:00 Boutin in / 14:00 out. 16:00 Vaillant in / 16:50 out. 17:30 Yvette in / 18:15 out. 19:05 Caillou in / 19:55 out.

A sixth entry, scratched with a different nib and stillborn of an exit time, reads merely: "? — second thought — no." The ink matches none of the five witnesses. The desk clerk, when questioned, claimed no memory of this interpolation. He did, however, recall that the book had been left unguarded for a quarter of an hour whilst he fetched his supper.

Set down under oath, and therefore no safer than any other vanity of men. A crabbed clerk's remembrance was found beneath the blotter: first bell, seventh stair, seventh lantern, seventh berth.

subtitle: Set down under oath, and therefore no safer than any other vanity of men.
